title: Trymalium wendlandianum
taxonomy: Trymalium wendlandianum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It is native to Australia and is commonly known as the 'Wendland's buckthorn'. The plant is classified within the genus Trymalium, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as Pomaderris. However, recent studies have reclassified these plants into the genus Trymalium. This classification change has helped to clarify the relationships between these plants and their place within the Rhamnaceae family.
